positioning.oneLiner: WebbAlign helps education teams use Depth of Knowledge accurately to support aligned, coherent systems.
positioning.summary: WebbAlign is a WCEPS program that works in collaboration with Dr. Norman Webb, developer of Depth of Knowledge. The program helps teams use DOK to evaluate standards, learning objectives, assessments, curricula, and instructional materials so learning opportunities and assessment tasks reflect the intended cognitive complexity.

audiences.name: curriculum teams
needs: Evaluate the alignment among standards, curriculum, assessments, and instructional materials
Structure professional conversations around cognitive complexity
approvedMessages: WebbAlign helps teams use the lens of DOK to evaluate standards, learning objectives, assessments, curricula, and instructional materials.
WebbAlign professional learning can help teams build a calibrated understanding of DOK to inform practice.
DOK supports conversations about cognitive complexity and coherent learning opportunities.

audiences.name: education leaders
needs: Understand where WebbAlign fits in a system improvement effort
Connect learning expectations, materials, and assessments
approvedMessages: WebbAlign promotes effective and accurate use of Depth of Knowledge language.
The program helps education teams work toward an aligned, coherent system.
WebbAlign is a WCEPS program connected to educational innovations that originated at UW-Madison.
ctaSuggestions: Learn how WebbAlign can support alignment

audiences.name: content developers
needs: Use DOK to inform item and task development
approvedMessages: DOK can inform instructional design and content development that elicits thinking aligned to learning targets.
WebbAlign supports analyses of educational materials.
WebbAlign works directly with Dr. Norman Webb, who developed the DOK language system.

proofPoints: The WebbAlign homepage identifies WebbAlign as a WCEPS program.
The WebbAlign site states that the team works in collaboration with Dr. Norman Webb.
The WebbAlign DOK page says DOK differentiates complexity from difficulty.
The districts and educators page describes online, onsite, and hybrid sessions tailored to team needs.
approvedPhrases: evaluate standards, learning objectives, assessments, curricula, and instructional materials
restrictedClaims: Do not imply WebbAlign guarantees assessment approval or compliance outcomes.
Do not describe DOK as a progression of instructional levels; the source says DOK definitions are not intended to represent progressions in learning or instructional levels.
Do not claim exclusive rights beyond the source statement that WebbAlign works directly with Dr. Norman Webb.

imageKeywords: clean cognitive complexity diagram without text

sourceEvidence.label: WebbAlign homepage positioning
sourceEvidence.excerpt: In collaboration with Dr. Norman Webb, the developer of Depth of Knowledge (DOK), we promote effective and accurate use of this innovative language system.

sourceEvidence.label: WebbAlign evaluation areas
sourceEvidence.excerpt: Our team helps you use the lens of DOK to evaluate standards, learning objectives, assessments, curricula, and instructional materials to work toward an aligned, or coherent, system.

sourceEvidence.label: Districts and educators page
sourceEvidence.excerpt: Structuring conversations about cognitive complexity through the lens of DOK leads to rich conversations that enhance understanding of academic expectations and tasks.

sourceEvidence.label: Professional learning format
sourceEvidence.excerpt: We offer online, onsite, and hybrid sessions tailored to your team's needs.

sourceEvidence.label: Webb's DOK explained
sourceEvidence.excerpt: Grounded in the language of the academic standards, the DOK framework differentiates complexity from difficulty.
